commits: 05b08af: docs(12-B): blocker report — benchmark-and-robustness substrate absent
jobs_dropped: go-fuzz-smoke (no go/ surface in 0.6.0 — definitive per ci-deferred.md)
agent_verify_result: not run — STOPPED at blocker surfacing per prompt instruction

evidence.scripts_run_benchmarks_sh: absent in current tree (ls scripts/ shows no run-benchmarks.sh)
evidence.fathomdb_benches: absent (src/rust/crates/fathomdb has no benches/ dir; Cargo.toml is pure facade, no [[bench]] entry, no criterion dep)
evidence.pre_0_6_0_script: git show 39ee271^:scripts/run-benchmarks.sh ran `cargo bench -p fathomdb --bench production_paths --features sqlite-vec` — neither bench nor feature exists in 0.6.0 facade crate

blockers_encountered.rationale_a_rejected: Pre-0.6.0 substrate ran against a wholly different Rust crate shape (single `fathomdb` crate with criterion benches + sqlite-vec feature). 0.6.0 facade crate is a re-export shell; resurrecting `production_paths` bench is net-new authorship of a benchmark harness against a redesigned engine, not restoration. Violates `feedback_reliability_principles` net-negative-LoC bias and `delete-before-add` discipline — that's a Pack 7 perf-evidence slice, explicitly out-of-scope per prompt header.
blockers_encountered.option_b_recommendation: Omit rust-benchmarks job entirely from initial workflow landing. Track restoration in ci-deferred.md as Pack 7 dependency.

evidence.engine_tests_dir: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/ — listed contents have no scale.rs (closest matches: durability_soak.rs, perf_gates.rs, neither is a `scale` test)
evidence.pre_0_6_0_invocation: cargo nextest run -p fathomdb --test scale (pre-0.6.0 crate name; renamed to fathomdb-engine in 0.6.0)

blockers_encountered.rationale_a_rejected: No scale.rs ever existed in the 0.6.0-rewrite engine substrate; authoring one is Pack 7 territory.
blockers_encountered.option_b_recommendation: Omit rust-scale-tests; or substitute `durability_soak` if orchestrator wants the slot occupied — though `durability_soak` violates `feedback_reliability_principles` (no soak). Recommend: drop.

evidence.engine_cargo_toml_features: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/Cargo.toml has no [features] section at all — `--features tracing` will fail with `Package fathomdb-engine v0.6.0 does not have feature 'tracing'`

blockers_encountered.rationale_a_rejected: Requires adding a cargo feature + writing a tracing-stress test against current engine — net-new production-surface work, not workflow restoration.
blockers_encountered.option_b_recommendation: Drop rust-tracing-stress job. Defer to whenever tracing-events instrumentation lands as a Phase 12+ slice.

evidence.tests_dir_listing: src/python/tests/: conftest.py, test_errors.py, test_ffi_safety.py, test_no_recovery_surface.py, test_property_template.py, test_save_time_validation.py, test_scaffold.py, test_surface.py, test_typed_errors.py — no test_stress.py

blockers_encountered.rationale_a_rejected: Pre-0.6.0 stress suite targeted the prior Python binding; semantics don't map onto current PyO3 surface without authoring a new harness.

evidence.src_ts_package_json: Single package `fathomdb`, NOT a workspace. No `workspaces` key. No sdk-harness subpackage.
evidence.scripts_shape: build:native runs napi build against ../rust/crates/fathomdb-napi; `test` script: `npm run build:debug && node --test dist/tests/*.test.js`
evidence.pre_0_6_0_invocation: npm run build --workspace @fathomdb/sdk-harness + npm test --workspace @fathomdb/sdk-harness — workspace structure does not exist in 0.6.0

blockers_encountered.rationale_a_rejected: Pre-0.6.0 had a multi-workspace npm layout (fathomdb + sdk-harness); 0.6.0 collapsed to a single package. Re-introducing the workspace + porting the harness is a Phase 11+ topology decision, not workflow restoration.

summary: All five would-be jobs in benchmark-and-robustness.yml depend on substrate that does not exist in the 0.6.0 codebase: a benchmark harness + script, a scale test target, a tracing cargo feature + stress test, a Python stress test file, and a TS sdk-harness workspace. The pre-0.6.0 workflow was a thin shell over substrate that was deleted during the rewrite. Per `feedback_reliability_principles` (net-negative LoC, delete-before-add) and the prompt's explicit out-of-scope note ("Any actual perf-evidence work (Pack 7 territory; this slice ships the workflow, not the experiments)"), authoring the missing harnesses is out-of-scope for 12-B. The shippable artifact reduces to either (a) a workflow with zero non-noop jobs (worse than no workflow — wastes a weekly cron slot for a green-by-vacuity result) or (b) a workflow shell with all five jobs commented out with TODO references (writes a placeholder that misleads future readers).
next_step_for_orchestrator: STOP 12-B as currently scoped. Orchestrator decision required between three paths: (1) Close 12-B as no-op and demote ci-deferred.md § benchmark-and-robustness.yml to a Pack 7 prerequisite (recommended — most honest). (2) Re-scope 12-B narrowly to ship the workflow with only jobs whose substrate exists (currently: zero) plus follow-up tickets per dropped job. (3) Expand 12-B to include substrate authorship for one or more jobs — exits Wave 1 parallel budget, becomes its own multi-slice initiative. Do not respawn codex reviewer until orchestrator picks a path; there is no implementation diff to review.
discipline_notes: Did not write a workflow yaml that would actionlint-green but reference non-existent test targets — actionlint validates syntax, not target existence, so a green actionlint on a workflow citing missing tests would be a false reassurance.
Did not stub bash scripts to satisfy `bash scripts/run-benchmarks.sh` — would violate prompt's 'Don't stub workflows that can't actually run' rule.
Did not invent cargo features or test files — would violate `feedback_reliability_principles` and exceed slice scope per the prompt's 'No data migration; no production code changes' bound.
